qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default-enabled downloadFrom fetcher permits unauthenticated SSRF and internal content exfiltration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the downloadFrom SSRF path and PDF exfiltration through /forms/chromium/convert/html, while the advisory targets ExifTool metadata filtering in /forms/pdfengines/metadata/write.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only real qualifying finding is an SSRF in the shared downloadFrom fetcher and Chromium HTML conversion flow. The advisory is a distinct ExifTool metadata-write blocklist bypass involving case-insensitive FileName or Directory tags and omitted HardLink or SymLink tags, so no qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: The advisory describes pkg/modules/exiftool/exiftool.go deleting only exact-case FileName and Directory keys and omitting HardLink and SymLink, enabling arbitrary file move or link creation through the metadata write API.
The qualifying finding report and PoC are scoped to attacker-controlled downloadFrom URLs, server-side GET requests, and exfiltration through POST /forms/chromium/convert/html.
The finding does not identify the ExifTool metadata-write path, the dangerous pseudo-tags, or the case-insensitive blocklist bypass described by the advisory.
